seo.title: Entities
seo.description: Entity layer patterns: presentation components, data models, and entity-level business logic organization

header.title: Business Domain (Entities)
header.description: Entities are the core business units of the project. This section describes the unified structure of any entity based on strict architectural rules.

benefit_text: Each business entity inside <one>src/entities/*</one> must follow a strict directory structure for logic and UI isolation.
modules_description: A complete entity consists of these required modules:

api.label: API definitions (RTK Query)
api.title: /api
api.description: Responsible exclusively for network interaction with the backend application within a single entity. It takes arguments for the request and returns raw <one>DTOs</one>, which must then be passed to converters.

converters.label: Data mappers (DTO <-> Domain)
converters.title: /converters
converters.description: Data mappers. They transform a raw server <one>DTO</one> into a convenient <two>Domain Model</two>. This isolates UI components from sudden changes in backend contracts.

handlers.label: MSW mock handlers
handlers.title: /handlers
handlers.description: <one>MSW (Mock Service Worker)</one> handlers. Used to intercept network calls during development and in integration tests (<two>Vitest</two> / <three>RTL</three>).

mock.label: Mock data
mock.title: /mock
mock.description: Static fixtures and fake data (<one>mock data</one>) used to emulate server responses in conjunction with <two>MSW handlers</two> or for <three>Storybook</three>.

schema.label: Zod validation schemas
schema.title: /schema
schema.description: <one>Single Source of Truth</one> for entity data types. Contains <two>Zod objects</two> with built-in internationalization (<three>i18n</three>) of errors.

slice.label: Redux state (if needed)
slice.title: /slice
slice.description: <one>Redux state</one> of the entity (if it stores global data). Selectors and actions often reside here as well. Note: state should depend solely on the <two>Domain Model</two>, not on <three>DTOs</three>.

ui.label: Presentation components
ui.title: /ui
ui.description: <one>Dump</one> or <two>Smart</two> components at the entity level. For example: <three>UserCard</three>, <four>UserAvatar</four>. Can be reused across different features and widgets.

types.label: Interface definitions
types.title: /types
types.description: Global TS interfaces and types for a specific entity (e.g., <one>Domain Model</one> interface if <two>Zod</two> doesn't cover all logic). Type inference from <three>Zod schemas</three> is recommended, but when not possible, types are defined here.
